steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option in the "New Project" modal dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface, as indicated by the red crosshair at coordinates (290, 230).
Click the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown menu within the "Teams Agent or App Using Microsoft Teams SDK" dialog in Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit on Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Simple Bot" option in the "Teams Capability" dropdown within Visual Studio Code’s Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface, selecting a template described as "A simple implementation of an echo bot that's ready for customization."
Click the "TypeScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" text field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog within Microsoft Visual Studio Code to select the project location for Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit.
Click the "Manage Microsoft 365 App (with Microsoft Entra app) Collaborators" button for the "dev (Provisioned)" environment in the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT side panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Add App Owners" option in the dropdown under "Manage who can make changes to your app" within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Agent Microsoft 365 Agent" option in the app selection dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code. The red crosshair marks the exact interaction point, ensuring the "Agent" app option is selected for managing collaborators.
Click the “Manage collaborators” blue button (with user icon) in the app selection popup of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "manifest.json" file option in the dropdown list within the "Select Teams manifest.json File" dialog of Visual Studio Code.
Select all text in the email input field of the "Add owner to Teams/Microsoft Entra app" dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.

Type the email address '${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_ACCOUNT}}' into the Teams/Microsoft Entra app owner input field within the account management modal in Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents extension.
Click on the email input field in the "Add owner to Teams/Microsoft Entra app" prompt within Visual Studio Code to activate it for account entry.

Press the Enter key to confirm the email input (${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_ACCOUNT}}) in the "Add owner to Teams/Microsoft Entra app" dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension for Visual Studio Code.
@assertion the notification "Added account" exists

Click the "Manage Microsoft 365 App (with Microsoft Entra app) Collaborators" button for the "dev (Provisioned)" environment in the ENVIRONMENT panel of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Add App Owners" option in the "Manage who can make changes to your app" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents extension panel in Visual Studio Code.

Click the "app YHP" option in the selection dropdown within the "Select the app for which you want to manage collaborators" dialog in Visual Studio Code, under the Microsoft 365 Agents extension panel.
Click the "OK" button in the app selection dialog to confirm the chosen app for managing collaborators within the Microsoft 365 Agents extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "manifest.json" entry in the file selection dropdown within the "Select Teams manifest.json File" dialog in Visual Studio Code.
@assertion there are two App Owners that listed in the output window
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code, within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace.

Type the command 'focus on accounts view' into the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit command palette search bar in Visual Studio Code, targeting the command suggestions dropdown at the top-center of the interface.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit: Focus on Accounts View" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette to trigger the Accounts View focus action.
Click the "Sign Out" button for the account ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}} in the ACCOUNTS section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit panel within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Sign out" button in the confirmation dialog to sign out of the account '${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}' within the Visual Studio Code Microsoft 365 extension interface.
@assertion notification "Successfully signed out" exists
Click the "Sign in to Microsoft 365" button in the ACCOUNTS section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit pane within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Sign in" button in the Microsoft 365 developer sandbox prompt within Visual Studio Code to begin authentication.

Click the "Use another account" option in the Microsoft account picker dialog on the login.microsoftonline.com sign-in page using Google Chrome. The click targets the area with a plus icon and label, below the listed account choices.
Paste clipboard content into the "Email or phone" input field on the Microsoft sign-in page within Google Chrome.
Click on the "Email or phone" input field in the Microsoft sign-in form on the login.microsoftonline.com page within Google Chrome.
Enter ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_ACCOUNT}} into the email or username input field on the Microsoft Sign in page within a Google Chrome browser window.
Click the "Next" button on the Microsoft sign-in page, positioned to the right of the "Back" button after entering the username ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_ACCOUNT}} in Google Chrome.
Type text ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_PASSWORD}} into the password input field on the Microsoft sign-in page for user ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_ACCOUNT}} within a Chrome browser window.
Click the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft login page in Google Chrome after entering the password for the account ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_ACCOUNT}}.
Click the "Close" (X) button at the top-right corner of the Chrome browser window to close the "Visual Studio Code - You are signed in now and can close this page" tab.
@assertion notification "Successfully signed in to Microsoft 365 account" exists
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code while the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" extension panel is active.
Type text 'provision' into the Visual Studio Code command palette textbox to search for commands within the Microsoft 365 Agents extension.
Select the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" command from the Visual Studio Code command palette by clicking on its entry at coordinates (440, 59).
Click the "Provision" button in the confirmation dialog prompting to provision resources for the selected Azure and Microsoft 365 accounts within the Visual Studio Code interface.
@assertion notification 'provision stage executed successfully' exist.
